{'url': 'https://dspy.ai/api/adapters/Adapter/', 'title': 'Adapter - DSPy', 'structure': [{'level': 1, 'title': 'dspy.Adapter', 'content': 'dspy.Adapter(callbacks:Optional[list[BaseCallback]]=None)Source code indspy/adapters/base.py1314def__init__(self,callbacks:Optional[list[BaseCallback]]=None):self.callbacks=callbacksor[]Functions__call__(lm:LM,lm_kwargs:dict[str,Any],signature:Type[Signature],demos:list[dict[str,Any]],inputs:dict[str,Any])->list[dict[str,Any]]Source code indspy/adapters/base.py232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849def__call__(self,lm:"LM",lm_kwargs:dict[str,Any],signature:Type[Signature],demos:list[dict[str,Any]],inputs:dict[str,Any],)->list[dict[str,Any]]:inputs=self.format(signature,demos,inputs)outputs=lm(messages=inputs,**lm_kwargs)values=[]foroutputinoutputs:output_logprobs=Noneifisinstance(output,dict):output,output_logprobs=output["text"],output["logprobs"]value=self.parse(signature,output)ifoutput_logprobsisnotNone:value["logprobs"]=output_logprobsvalues.append(value)returnvaluesformat(signature:Type[Signature],demos:list[dict[str,Any]],inputs:dict[str,Any])->list[dict[str,Any]]Format the input messages for the LM call.This method converts the DSPy structured input along with few-shot examples and conversation history into\nmultiturn messages as expected by the LM. For custom adapters, this method can be overridden to customize\nthe formatting of the input messages.In general we recommend the messages to have the following structure:[{"role": "system", "content": system_message},# Begin few-shot examples{"role": "user", "content": few_shot_example_1_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": few_shot_example_1_output},{"role": "user", "content": few_shot_example_2_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": few_shot_example_2_output},...# End few-shot examples# Begin conversation history{"role": "user", "content": conversation_history_1_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": conversation_history_1_output},{"role": "user", "content": conversation_history_2_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": conversation_history_2_output},...# End conversation history{"role": "user", "content": current_input},]And system message should contain the field description, field structure, and task description.Parameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultsignatureType[Signature]The DSPy signature for which to format the input messages.requireddemoslist[dict[str,Any]]A list of few-shot examples.requiredinputsdict[str,Any]The input arguments to the DSPy module.requiredReturns:TypeDescriptionlist[dict[str,Any]]A list of multiturn messages as expected by the LM.Source code indspy/adapters/base.py51525354555657585960616263646566676869707172737475767778798081828384858687888990919293949596979899100101102103104105106107108109110111112113114115116117118119120121122123124125126127128129defformat(self,signature:Type[Signature],demos:list[dict[str,Any]],inputs:dict[str,Any],)->list[dict[str,Any]]:"""Format the input messages for the LM call.This method converts the DSPy structured input along with few-shot examples and conversation history intomultiturn messages as expected by the LM. For custom adapters, this method can be overridden to customizethe formatting of the input messages.In general we recommend the messages to have the following structure:```[{"role": "system", "content": system_message},# Begin few-shot examples{"role": "user", "content": few_shot_example_1_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": few_shot_example_1_output},{"role": "user", "content": few_shot_example_2_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": few_shot_example_2_output},...# End few-shot examples# Begin conversation history{"role": "user", "content": conversation_history_1_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": conversation_history_1_output},{"role": "user", "content": conversation_history_2_input},{"role": "assistant", "content": conversation_history_2_output},...# End conversation history{"role": "user", "content": current_input},]And system message should contain the field description, field structure, and task description.```Args:signature: The DSPy signature for which to format the input messages.demos: A list of few-shot examples.inputs: The input arguments to the DSPy module.Returns:A list of multiturn messages as expected by the LM."""inputs_copy=dict(inputs)# If the signature and inputs have conversation history, we need to format the conversation history and# remove the history field from the signature.history_field_name=self._get_history_field_name(signature)ifhistory_field_name:# In order to format the conversation history, we need to remove the history field from the signature.signature_without_history=signature.delete(history_field_name)conversation_history=self.format_conversation_history(signature_without_history,history_field_name,inputs_copy,)messages=[]system_message=(f"{self.format_field_description(signature)}\\n"f"{self.format_field_structure(signature)}\\n"f"{self.format_task_description(signature)}")messages.append({"role":"system","content":system_message})messages.extend(self.format_demos(signature,demos))ifhistory_field_name:# Conversation history and current inputmessages.extend(conversation_history)messages.append({"role":"user","content":self.format_user_message_content(signature_without_history,inputs_copy)})else:# Only current inputmessages.append({"role":"user","content":self.format_user_message_content(signature,inputs_copy)})messages=try_expand_image_tags(messages)returnmessagesformat_conversation_history(signature:Type[Signature],history_field_name:str,inputs:dict[str,Any])->list[dict[str,Any]]Format There are two modes:\n- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\nthe file extension.\n- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\narchitecture of the model.\nWe also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.\nArgs:\npath (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,\nand a directory when `save_program=True`.\nsave_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.\n"""\nmetadata\n=\n{}\nmetadata\n[\n"dependency_versions"\n]\n=\nget_dependency_versions\n()\npath\n=\nPath\n(\npath\n)\nif\nsave_program\n:\nif\npath\n.\nsuffix\n:\nraise\nValueError\n(\nf\n"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:\n{\npath\n}\n"\n)\nif\npath\n.\nexists\n()\nand\nnot\npath\n.\nis_dir\n():\nraise\nNotADirectoryError\n(\nf\n"The path \'\n{\npath\n}\n\' exists but is not a directory."\n)\nif\nnot\npath\n.\nexists\n():\n# Create the directory (and any parent directories)\npath\n.\nmkdir\n(\nparents\n=\nTrue\n)\ntry\n:\nwith\nopen\n(\npath\n/\n"program.pkl"\n,\n"wb"\n)\nas\nf\n:\ncloudpickle\n.\ndump\n(\nself\n,\nf\n)\nexcept\nException\nas\ne\n:\nraise\nRuntimeError\n(\nf\n"Saving failed with error:\n{\ne\n}\n. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. default python deepcopy that only deep copies `self.parameters()`, and for otherattributes, we just do the shallow copy."""try:# If the instance itself is copyable, we can just deep copy it.# Otherwise we will have to create a new instance and copy over the attributes one by one.returncopy.deepcopy(self)exceptException:pass# Create an empty instance.new_instance=self.__class__.__new__(self.__class__)# Set attribuetes of the copied instance.forattr,valueinself.__dict__.items():ifisinstance(value,BaseModule):setattr(new_instance,attr,value.deepcopy())else:try:# Try to deep copy the reference.setattr(new_instance,attr,value)returnnew_instancedump_state()Source code There are two modes:\n-save_program=False: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\n    the file extension.\n-save_program=True: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\n    architecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Parameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultpathstrPath to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file whensave_program=False,\nand a directory whensave_program=True.requiredsave_programboolIf True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.FalseSource code indspy/primitives/module.py166167168169170171172173174175176177178179180181182183184185186187188189190191192193194195196197198199200201202203204205206207208209210211212213214215216217218219220221222223224225226227228229defsave(self,path,save_program=False):"""Save the module.Save the module to a directory or a file. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. Please remove the non-picklable attributes from your DSPy program, ""or consider using state-only saving by setting `save_program=False`.")withopen(path/"metadata.json","w")asf:ujson.dump(metadata,f,indent=2)returnstate=self.dump_state()state["metadata"]=metadataifpath.suffix==".json":try:withopen(path,"w")asf:f.write(ujson.dumps(state,indent=2))exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Failed to save state to{path}with error:{e}. This class provides a unified interface for both:Hosted embedding models (e.g. OpenAI\'s text-embedding-3-small) via litellm integrationCustom embedding functions that you provideFor hosted models, simply pass the model name as a string (e.g., "openai/text-embedding-3-small"). The class will use\nlitellm to handle the API calls and caching.For custom embedding models, pass a callable function that:\n- Takes a list of strings as input.\n- Returns embeddings as either:\n    - A 2D numpy array of float32 values\n    - A 2D list of float32 values\n- Each row should represent one embedding vectorParameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultmodelThe embedding model to use. There are two modes:\n-save_program=False: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\n    the file extension.\n-save_program=True: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\n    architecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Parameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultpathstrPath to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file whensave_program=False,\nand a directory whensave_program=True.requiredsave_programboolIf True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.FalseSource code indspy/primitives/module.py166167168169170171172173174175176177178179180181182183184185186187188189190191192193194195196197198199200201202203204205206207208209210211212213214215216217218219220221222223224225226227228229defsave(self,path,save_program=False):"""Save the module.Save the module to a directory or a file. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. There are two modes:\n-save_program=False: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\n    the file extension.\n-save_program=True: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\n    architecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Parameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultpathstrPath to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file whensave_program=False,\nand a directory whensave_program=True.requiredsave_programboolIf True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.FalseSource code indspy/primitives/module.py166167168169170171172173174175176177178179180181182183184185186187188189190191192193194195196197198199200201202203204205206207208209210211212213214215216217218219220221222223224225226227228229defsave(self,path,save_program=False):"""Save the module.Save the module to a directory or a file. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. Please remove the non-picklable attributes from your DSPy program, ""or consider using state-only saving by setting `save_program=False`.")withopen(path/"metadata.json","w")asf:ujson.dump(metadata,f,indent=2)returnstate=self.dump_state()state["metadata"]=metadataifpath.suffix==".json":try:withopen(path,"w")asf:f.write(ujson.dumps(state,indent=2))exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Failed to save state to{path}with error:{e}. module.This is a tweak to the default python deepcopy that only deep copies `self.parameters()`, and for otherattributes, we just do the shallow copy."""try:# If the instance itself is copyable, we Defaults to N if not provided.\nExample:\n```python\nimport dspy\ndspy.settings.configure(lm=dspy.LM("openai/gpt-4o-mini"))\n# Define a QA module with chain of thought\nqa = dspy.ChainOfThought("question -> answer")\n# Define a reward function that checks for one-word answers\ndef one_word_answer(args, pred):\nreturn 1.0 if len(pred.answer.split()) == 1 else 0.0\n# Create a refined module that tries up to 3 times\nbest_of_3 = dspy.BestOfN(module=qa, N=3, reward_fn=one_word_answer, threshold=1.0)\n# Use the refined module\nresult = best_of_3(question="What is the capital of Belgium?").answer\n# Returns: Brussels\n```\n"""\nself\n.\nmodule\n=\nmodule\nself\n.\nreward_fn\n=\nlambda\n*\nargs\n:\nreward_fn\n(\n*\nargs\n)\n# to prevent this from becoming a There are two modes:\n- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\nthe file extension.\n- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\narchitecture of the model.\nWe also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.\nArgs:\npath (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,\nand a directory when `save_program=True`.\nsave_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.\n"""\nmetadata\n=\n{}\nmetadata\n[\n"dependency_versions"\n]\n=\nget_dependency_versions\n()\npath\n=\nPath\n(\npath\n)\nif\nsave_program\n:\nif\npath\n.\nsuffix\n:\nraise\nValueError\n(\nf\n"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:\n{\npath\n}\n"\n)\nif\npath\n.\nexists\n()\nand\nnot\npath\n.\nis_dir\n():\nraise\nNotADirectoryError\n(\nf\n"The path \'\n{\npath\n}\n\' exists but is not a directory."\n)\nif\nnot\npath\n.\nexists\n():\n# Create the directory (and any parent directories)\npath\n.\nmkdir\n(\nparents\n=\nTrue\n)\ntry\n:\nwith\nopen\n(\npath\n/\n"program.pkl"\n,\n"wb"\n)\nas\nf\n:\ncloudpickle\n.\ndump\n(\nself\n,\nf\n)\nexcept\nException\nas\ne\n:\nraise\nRuntimeError\n(\nf\n"Saving failed with error:\n{\ne\n}\n. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. There are two modes:\n- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\nthe file extension.\n- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\narchitecture of the model.\nWe also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.\nArgs:\npath (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,\nand a directory when `save_program=True`.\nsave_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.\n"""\nmetadata\n=\n{}\nmetadata\n[\n"dependency_versions"\n]\n=\nget_dependency_versions\n()\npath\n=\nPath\n(\npath\n)\nif\nsave_program\n:\nif\npath\n.\nsuffix\n:\nraise\nValueError\n(\nf\n"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:\n{\npath\n}\n"\n)\nif\npath\n.\nexists\n()\nand\nnot\npath\n.\nis_dir\n():\nraise\nNotADirectoryError\n(\nf\n"The path \'\n{\npath\n}\n\' exists but is not a directory."\n)\nif\nnot\npath\n.\nexists\n():\n# Create the directory (and any parent directories)\npath\n.\nmkdir\n(\nparents\n=\nTrue\n)\ntry\n:\nwith\nopen\n(\npath\n/\n"program.pkl"\n,\n"wb"\n)\nas\nf\n:\ncloudpickle\n.\ndump\n(\nself\n,\nf\n)\nexcept\nException\nas\ne\n:\nraise\nRuntimeError\n(\nf\n"Saving failed with error:\n{\ne\n}\n. There are two modes:\n-save_program=False: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\n    the file extension.\n-save_program=True: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\n    architecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Parameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultpathstrPath to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file whensave_program=False,\nand a directory whensave_program=True.requiredsave_programboolIf True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.FalseSource code indspy/primitives/module.py166167168169170171172173174175176177178179180181182183184185186187188189190191192193194195196197198199200201202203204205206207208209210211212213214215216217218219220221222223224225226227228229defsave(self,path,save_program=False):"""Save the module.Save the module to a directory or a file. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. Please remove the non-picklable attributes from your DSPy program, ""or consider using state-only saving by setting `save_program=False`.")withopen(path/"metadata.json","w")asf:ujson.dump(metadata,f,indent=2)returnstate=self.dump_state()state["metadata"]=metadataifpath.suffix==".json":try:withopen(path,"w")asf:f.write(ujson.dumps(state,indent=2))exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Failed to save state to{path}with error:{e}. Your DSPy program may contain non ""json-serializable objects, please consider saving the state in .pkl by using `path` ending ""with `.pkl`, or saving the whole program by setting There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. the module.\nSave the module to a directory or a file. There are two modes:\n-\nsave_program=False\n: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\n    the file extension.\n-\nsave_program=True\n: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\n    architecture of the model.\nWe also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.\nParameters:\nName\nType\nDescription\nDefault\npath\nstr\nPath to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when\nsave_program=False\n,\nand a directory when\nsave_program=True\n.\nrequired\nsave_program\nbool\nIf True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.\nFalse\nSource code in\ndspy/primitives/module.py\n166\n167\n168\n169\n170\n171\n172\n173\n174\n175\n176\n177\n178\n179\n180\n181\n182\n183\n184\n185\n186\n187\n188\n189\n190\n191\n192\n193\n194\n195\n196\n197\n198\n199\n200\n201\n202\n203\n204\n205\n206\n207\n208\n209\n210\n211\n212\n213\n214\n215\n216\n217\n218\n219\n220\n221\n222\n223\n224\n225\n226\n227\n228\n229\ndef\nsave\n(\nself\n,\npath\n,\nsave_program\n=\nFalse\n):\n"""Save the module.\nSave the module to a directory or a file. There are two modes:\n- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\nthe file extension.\n- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\narchitecture of the model.\nWe also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.\nArgs:\npath (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,\nand a directory when `save_program=True`.\nsave_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.\n"""\nmetadata\n=\n{}\nmetadata\n[\n"dependency_versions"\n]\n=\nget_dependency_versions\n()\npath\n=\nPath\n(\npath\n)\nif\nsave_program\n:\nif\npath\n.\nsuffix\n:\nraise\nValueError\n(\nf\n"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:\n{\npath\n}\n"\n)\nif\npath\n.\nexists\n()\nand\nnot\npath\n.\nis_dir\n():\nraise\nNotADirectoryError\n(\nf\n"The path \'\n{\npath\n}\n\' exists but is not a directory."\n)\nif\nnot\npath\n.\nexists\n():\n# Create the directory (and any parent directories)\npath\n.\nmkdir\n(\nparents\n=\nTrue\n)\ntry\n:\nwith\nopen\n(\npath\n/\n"program.pkl"\n,\n"wb"\n)\nas\nf\n:\ncloudpickle\n.\ndump\n(\nself\n,\nf\n)\nexcept\nException\nas\ne\n:\nraise\nRuntimeError\n(\nf\n"Saving failed with error:\n{\ne\n}\n. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. Please remove the non-picklable attributes from your DSPy program, ""or consider using state-only saving by setting `save_program=False`.")withopen(path/"metadata.json","w")asf:ujson.dump(metadata,f,indent=2)returnstate=self.dump_state()state["metadata"]=metadataifpath.suffix==".json":try:withopen(path,"w")asf:f.write(ujson.dumps(state,indent=2))exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Failed to save state to{path}with error:{e}. Your DSPy program may contain non ""json-serializable objects, please consider saving the state in .pkl by using `path` ending ""with `.pkl`, or saving the whole program by setting `save_program=True`.")elifpath.suffix==".pkl":withopen(path,"wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(state,f)else:raiseValueError(f"`path` must end with `.json` or `.pkl` when `save_program=False`, but a list of dspy.Example instances in parallel using the Parallel module.:param examples: List of dspy.Example instances to process.\n:param num_threads: Number of threads to use for parallel processing.\n:param max_errors: Maximum number of errors allowed before stopping execution.\n:param return_failed_examples: Whether to return failed examples and exceptions.\n:param provide_traceback: indspy/primitives/program.py9293949596979899100101102103104105106107108109110111112113114115116117118119120121122123124125126127128129defbatch(self,examples,num_threads:int=32,max_errors:int=10,return_failed_examples:bool=False,provide_traceback:bool=False,disable_progress_bar:bool=False,):"""Processes a list of dspy.Example instances in parallel using the Parallel module.:param examples: List of dspy.Example instances to process.:param num_threads: Number of threads to use for parallel processing.:param max_errors: Maximum number of errors allowed before stopping execution.:param return_failed_examples: Whether to return failed examples and exceptions.:param provide_traceback: Whether to include traceback information in error logs.:return: List of results, and optionally failed examples and exceptions."""# Create a list of execution pairs (self, example)exec_pairs=[(self,example.inputs())forexampleinexamples]# Create an instance of Parallelparallel_executor=Parallel(num_threads=num_threads,max_errors=max_errors,return_failed_examples=return_failed_examples,provide_traceback=provide_traceback,disable_progress_bar=disable_progress_bar,)# Execute the forward method of Parallelifreturn_failed_examples:results,failed_examples,exceptions=parallel_executor.forward(exec_pairs)returnresults,failed_examples,exceptionselse:results=parallel_executor.forward(exec_pairs)returnresults\n\ndeepcopy()Deep copy the module.This is a tweak to the default python deepcopy that only deep copiesself.parameters(), and for other\nattributes, we just do the shallow copy.Source code indspy/primitives/module.py113114115116117118119120121122123124125126127128129130131132133134135136137138139140141142143144145146147148defdeepcopy(self):"""Deep copy the module.This is a tweak to the default python deepcopy that only deep copies `self.parameters()`, and for otherattributes, we just do the shallow copy."""try:# If the instance itself is copyable, we can just deep copy it.# Otherwise we will have to create a new instance and copy over the attributes one by one.returncopy.deepcopy(self)exceptException:pass# Create an empty instance.new_instance=self.__class__.__new__(self.__class__)# Set attribuetes of the copied instance.forattr,valueinself.__dict__.items():ifisinstance(value,BaseModule):setattr(new_instance,attr,value.deepcopy())else:try:# Try to deep copy the attributesetattr(new_instance,attr,copy.deepcopy(value))exceptException:logging.warning(f"Failed to deep copy attribute \'{attr}\' of{self.__class__.__name__}, ""falling back to shallow copy or reference copy.")try:# Fallback to shallow copy if deep copy failssetattr(new_instance,attr,copy.copy(value))exceptException:# If even the shallow copy fails, we just copy over the reference.setattr(new_instance,attr,value)returnnew_instance\n\ndump_state()Source code There are two modes:\n- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\nthe file extension.\n- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\narchitecture of the model.\nWe also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.\nArgs:\npath (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,\nand a directory when `save_program=True`.\nsave_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.\n"""\nmetadata\n=\n{}\nmetadata\n[\n"dependency_versions"\n]\n=\nget_dependency_versions\n()\npath\n=\nPath\n(\npath\n)\nif\nsave_program\n:\nif\npath\n.\nsuffix\n:\nraise\nValueError\n(\nf\n"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:\n{\npath\n}\n"\n)\nif\npath\n.\nexists\n()\nand\nnot\npath\n.\nis_dir\n():\nraise\nNotADirectoryError\n(\nf\n"The path \'\n{\npath\n}\n\' exists but is not a directory."\n)\nif\nnot\npath\n.\nexists\n():\n# Create the directory (and any parent directories)\npath\n.\nmkdir\n(\nparents\n=\nTrue\n)\ntry\n:\nwith\nopen\n(\npath\n/\n"program.pkl"\n,\n"wb"\n)\nas\nf\n:\ncloudpickle\n.\ndump\n(\nself\n,\nf\n)\nexcept\nException\nas\ne\n:\nraise\nRuntimeError\n(\nf\n"Saving failed with error:\n{\ne\n}\n. default python deepcopy that only deep copies `self.parameters()`, and for otherattributes, we just do the shallow copy."""try:# If the instance itself is copyable, we can just deep copy it.# There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. copy the module.This is a tweak to the default python deepcopy that only deep copiesself.parameters(), and for other\nattributes, we just do the shallow copy.Source code indspy/primitives/module.py113114115116117118119120121122123124125126127128129130131132133134135136137138139140141142143144145146147148defdeepcopy(self):"""Deep copy the module.This is a tweak to the default python deepcopy that only deep copies `self.parameters()`, and for otherattributes, we just do the shallow copy."""try:# If the instance itself is copyable, we can just deep copy it.# Otherwise we will have to create a new instance and copy over the attributes one by one.returncopy.deepcopy(self)exceptException:pass# Create an empty instance.new_instance=self.__class__.__new__(self.__class__)# Set attribuetes of the copied instance.forattr,valueinself.__dict__.items():ifisinstance(value,BaseModule):setattr(new_instance,attr,value.deepcopy())else:try:# Try to deep copy the attributesetattr(new_instance,attr,copy.deepcopy(value))exceptException:logging.warning(f"Failed to deep copy attribute \'{attr}\' of{self.__class__.__name__}, ""falling back to shallow copy or reference copy.")try:# Fallback to shallow copy if deep copy failssetattr(new_instance,attr,copy.copy(value))exceptException:# If even the shallow copy fails, we just copy over the reference.setattr(new_instance,attr,value)returnnew_instancedump_state()Source code Whether to include traceback information in error logs.:return: List of results, and optionally failed examples and exceptions."""# Create a list of execution pairs (self, example)exec_pairs=[(self,example.inputs())forexampleinexamples]# Create an instance of Parallelparallel_executor=Parallel(num_threads=num_threads,max_errors=max_errors,return_failed_examples=return_failed_examples,provide_traceback=provide_traceback,disable_progress_bar=disable_progress_bar,)# Execute the forward method of Parallelifreturn_failed_examples:results,failed_examples,exceptions=parallel_executor.forward(exec_pairs)returnresults,failed_examples,exceptionselse:results=parallel_executor.forward(exec_pairs)returnresults\n\ndeepcopy()Deep default python deepcopy that only deep copies `self.parameters()`, and for otherattributes, we just do the shallow copy."""try:# If the instance itself is copyable, we can just deep copy it.# There are two modes:\n-save_program=False: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value of\n    the file extension.\n-save_program=True: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state and\n    architecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch on\ncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Parameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultpathstrPath to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file whensave_program=False,\nand a directory whensave_program=True.requiredsave_programboolIf True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only save\nthe state.FalseSource code indspy/primitives/module.py166167168169170171172173174175176177178179180181182183184185186187188189190191192193194195196197198199200201202203204205206207208209210211212213214215216217218219220221222223224225226227228229defsave(self,path,save_program=False):"""Save the module.Save the module to a directory or a file. There are two modes:- `save_program=False`: Save only the state of the module to a json or pickle file, based on the value ofthe file extension.- `save_program=True`: Save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, which contains both the state andarchitecture of the model.We also save the dependency versions, so that the loaded model can check if there is a version mismatch oncritical dependencies or DSPy version.Args:path (str): Path to the saved state file, which should be a .json or .pkl file when `save_program=False`,and a directory when `save_program=True`.save_program (bool): If True, save the whole module to a directory via cloudpickle, otherwise only savethe state."""metadata={}metadata["dependency_versions"]=get_dependency_versions()path=Path(path)ifsave_program:ifpath.suffix:raiseValueError(f"`path` must point to a directory without a suffix when `save_program=True`, but received:{path}")ifpath.exists()andnotpath.is_dir():raiseNotADirectoryError(f"The path \'{path}\' exists but is not a directory.")ifnotpath.exists():# Create the directory (and any parent directories)path.mkdir(parents=True)try:withopen(path/"program.pkl","wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(self,f)exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Saving failed with error:{e}. Please remove the non-picklable attributes from your DSPy program, ""or consider using state-only saving by setting `save_program=False`.")withopen(path/"metadata.json","w")asf:ujson.dump(metadata,f,indent=2)returnstate=self.dump_state()state["metadata"]=metadataifpath.suffix==".json":try:withopen(path,"w")asf:f.write(ujson.dumps(state,indent=2))exceptExceptionase:raiseRuntimeError(f"Failed to save state to{path}with error:{e}. Your DSPy program may contain non ""json-serializable objects, please consider saving the state in .pkl by using `path` ending ""with `.pkl`, or saving the whole program by setting `save_program=True`.")elifpath.suffix==".pkl":withopen(path,"wb")asf:cloudpickle.dump(state,f)else:raiseValueError(f"`path` must end with `.json` or `.pkl` when `save_program=False`, but received:{path}")\n\nset_lm(lm)Source code indspy/primitives/program.py404142defset_lm(self,lm):for_,paraminself.named_predictors():param.lm=lm\n\ntruncate_trajectory(trajectory)Truncates the For each input example in a forward call, it identifies the k most\nsimilar examples from the trainset and attaches them as demonstrations to the student module.Parameters:NameTypeDescriptionDefaultkintThe number of nearest neighbors to attach to the student model.requiredtrainsetlist[Example]The training set to use for few-shot prompting.requiredvectorizerEmbedderTheEmbedderto use for vectorizationrequired**few_shot_bootstrap_argsAdditional arguments for theBootstrapFewShotoptimizer.{}Exampleimportdspyfromsentence_transformersimportSentenceTransformer# Define a QA module with chain of thoughtqa=dspy.ChainOfThought("question -> answer")# Create a training dataset with examplestrainset=[dspy.Example(question="What is the capital of France?",answer="Paris").with_inputs("question"),# ... more examples ...]# Initialize KNNFewShot with a sentence transformer modelknn_few_shot=KNNFewShot(k=3,trainset=trainset,vectorizer=dspy.Embedder(SentenceTransformer("all-MiniLM-L6-v2").encode))# Compile the QA module with few-shot learningcompiled_qa=knn_few_shot.compile(qa)# Use the compiled moduleresult=compiled_qa("What is the capital of Belgium?")Source code indspy/teleprompt/knn_fewshot.py121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950515253def__init__(self,k:int,trainset:list[Example],vectorizer:Embedder,**few_shot_bootstrap_args):"""KNNFewShot is an optimizer that uses an in-memory KNN retriever to find the k nearest neighborsin a trainset at test time. DSPy 2.6.\nBestofN\nRuns a module up to\nN\ntimes with different temperatures and returns the best prediction, as defined by the\nreward_fn\n, or the first prediction that passes the\nthreshold\n.\nimport\ndspy\nqa\n=\ndspy\n.\nChainOfThought\n(\n"question -> is the capital of Belgium?"\n)\n.\nanswer\n# Brussels\nRefine\nRefines a module by running it up to\nN\ntimes with different temperatures and returns the best prediction, as defined by the\nreward_fn\n, or the first prediction that passes the\nthreshold\n. After each attempt (except the final one),\nRefine\nautomatically generates detailed feedback about the module\'s performance and uses this feedback as hints for subsequent runs, creating an iterative refinement process.\nimport\ndspy\nqa\n=\ndspy\n.\nChainOfThought\n(\n"question -> answer"\n)\ndef\none_word_answer\n(\nargs\n,\npred\n):\nreturn\n1.0\nif\nlen\n(\npred\n.\nanswer\n)\n==\n1\nelse\n0.0\nbest_of_3\n=\ndspy\n.\nRefine\n(\nmodule\n=\nqa\n,\nN\n=\n3\n,\nreward_fn\n=\none_word_answer\n,\nthreshold\n=\n1.0\n)\nbest_of_3\n(\nquestion\n=\n"What is the capital of Belgium?"\n)\n.\nanswer\n# Brussels\nError Handling\nBy default,\nRefine\nwill try to run the module up to N times until the threshold is met. If the module encounters an error, it will keep going up to N failed attempts. You can change this behavior by setting\nfail_count\nto a smaller number than\nN\n.\nrefine\n=\ndspy\n.\nRefine\n(\nmodule\n=\nqa\n,\nN\n=\n3\n,\nreward_fn\n=\none_word_answer\n,\nthreshold\n=\n1.0\n,\nfail_count\n=\n1\n)\n...\nrefine\n(\nquestion\n=\n"What is the capital of Belgium?"\n)\n# If we encounter just one failed attempt, the module will raise an error.\nIf you want to run the module up to N times without any error handling, you can set\nfail_count\nto\nN\n. This is the default behavior.\nrefine\n=\ndspy\n.\nRefine\n(\nmodule\n=\nqa\n,\nN\n=\n3\n,\nreward_fn\n=\none_word_answer\n,\nthreshold\n=\n1.0\n,\nfail_count\n=\n3\n)\n...\nrefine\n(\nquestion\n=\n"What is the capital of Belgium?"\n)', 'last_updated': '2025-04-11T17:40:28.190430'}

If you're interested in contributing, please reach out to us on Discord."}, {'level': 2, 'title': 'Contributing', 'content': "What if I have a better idea for prompting or synthetic data generation?Perfect. We encourage you to think if it's best expressed as a module or an optimizer, and we'd love to merge it in DSPy so everyone can use it. DSPy is not a complete project; it's an ongoing effort to create structure (modules and optimizers) in place of hacky prompt and pipeline engineering tricks.\n\nHow can I add my favorite LM or vector store?\n\nCheck out these walkthroughs on setting up aCustom LM clientandCustom RM client."}], 'full_text': "Contributing\nDevelopment Setup\nWe are working on a development setup guide. If you're interested in contributing, please reach out to us on Discord.\nContributing\nWhat if I have a better idea for prompting or synthetic data generation?\nPerfect. We encourage you to think if it's best expressed as a module or an optimizer, and we'd love to merge it in DSPy so everyone can use it. DSPy is not a complete project; it's an ongoing effort to create structure (modules and optimizers) in place of hacky prompt and pipeline engineering tricks.\nHow can I add my favorite LM or vector store?\nCheck out these walkthroughs on setting up a\nCustom LM client\nand\nCustom RM client\n.", 'last_updated': '2025-04-11T17:41:56.243260'}

{'url': 'https://dspy.ai/deep-dive/retrieval_models_clients/Azure/', 'title': 'Azure - DSPy', 'structure': [{'level': 1, 'title': 'AzureAISearch', 'content': 'A retrieval module that utilizes Azure AI Search to retrieve top passages for a given query.'}, {'level': 2, 'title': 'Prerequisites', 'content': 'pipinstallazure-search-documents'}, {'level': 2, 'title': 'Setting up the AzureAISearchRM Client', 'content': 'The constructor initializes an instance of theAzureAISearchRMclass and sets up parameters for sending queries and retrieving results with the Azure AI Search server.\n\nsearch_service_name(str): The name of the Azure AI Search service.search_api_key(str): The API key for accessing the Azure AI Search service.search_index_name(str): The name of the search index in the Azure AI Search service.field_text(str): The name of the field containing text content in the search index. This field will be mapped to the "content" field in the dsp framework.field_vector(Optional[str]): The name of the field containing vector content in the search index.k(int, optional): The default number of top passages to retrieve. Defaults to 3.azure_openai_client(Optional[openai.AzureOpenAI]): An instance of the AzureOpenAI client. Either openai_client or embedding_func must be provided. Defaults to None.openai_embed_model(Optional[str]): The name of the OpenAI embedding model. Defaults to "text-embedding-ada-002".embedding_func(Optional[Callable]): A function for generating embeddings. Either openai_client or embedding_func must be provided. Defaults to None.semantic_ranker(bool, optional): Whether to use semantic ranking. Defaults to False.filter(str, optional): Additional filter query. Defaults to None.query_language(str, optional): The language of the query. Defaults to "en-Us".query_speller(str, optional): The speller mode. Defaults to "lexicon".use_semantic_captions(bool, optional): Whether to use semantic captions. Defaults to False.query_type(Optional[QueryType], optional): The type of query. Defaults to QueryType.FULL.semantic_configuration_name(str, optional): The name of the semantic configuration. Defaults to None.is_vector_search(Optional[bool]): Whether to enable vector search. Defaults to False.is_hybrid_search(Optional[bool]): Whether to enable hybrid search. Defaults to False.is_fulltext_search(Optional[bool]): Whether to enable fulltext search. Defaults to True.vector_filter_mode(Optional[VectorFilterMode]): The vector filter mode. Defaults to None.\n\nAvailable Query Types:\n\nSIMPLE\n    """Uses the simple query syntax for searches. Search text is interpreted using a simple query\n    #: language that allows for symbols such as +, * and "". Queries are evaluated across all\n    #: searchable fields by default, unless the searchFields parameter is specified."""FULL\n    """Uses the full Lucene query syntax for searches. Search text is interpreted using the Lucene\n    #: query language which allows field-specific and weighted searches, as well as other advanced\n    #: features."""SEMANTIC\n    """Best suited for queries expressed in natural language as opposed to keywords. Improves\n    #: precision of search results by re-ranking the top search results using a ranking model trained\n    #: on the Web corpus.""More Details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/search-query-overview\n\nAvailable Vector Filter Mode:\n\nPOST_FILTER = "postFilter"\n    """The filter will be applied after the candidate set of vector results is returned. Depending on\n    #: the filter selectivity, this can result in fewer results than requested by the parameter \'k\'."""PRE_FILTER = "preFilter"\n    """The filter will be applied before the search query."""More Details: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/search/vector-search-filters\n\nNote\n\nTheAzureAISearchRMclient allows you to perform Vector search, Hybrid search, or Full text search.By default, theAzureAISearchRMclient uses the Azure OpenAI Client for generating embeddings. If you want to use something else, you can provide your custom embedding_func, but either the openai_client or embedding_func must be provided.If you need to enable semantic search, either with vector, hybrid, or full text search, then set thesemantic_rankerflag to True.Ifsemantic_rankeris True, always set thequery_typeto QueryType.SEMANTIC and always provide thesemantic_configuration_name.\n\nExample of the AzureAISearchRM constructor:\n\nAzureAISearchRM(search_service_name:str,search_api_key:str,search_index_name:str,field_text:str,field_vector:Optional[str]=None,k:int=3,azure_openai_client:Optional[openai.AzureOpenAI]=None,openai_embed_model:Optional[str]="text-embedding-ada-002",embedding_func:Optional[Callable]=None,semantic_ranker:bool=False,filter:str=None,query_language:str="en-Us",query_speller:str="lexicon",use_semantic_captions:bool=False,query_type:Optional[QueryType]=QueryType.FULL,semantic_configuration_name:str=None,is_vector_search:Optional[bool]=False,is_hybrid_search:Optional[bool]=False,is_fulltext_search:Optional[bool]=True,vector_filter_mode:Optional[VectorFilterMode.PRE_FILTER]=None)'}, {'level': 2, 'title': 'Under the Hood', 'content': ''}, {'level': 3, 'title': 'forward(self, query_or_queries: Union[str, List[str]], k: Optional[int] = None) -> dspy.Prediction', 'content': 'Parameters:\n\nquery_or_queries(Union[str, List[str]]): The query or queries to search for.k(Optional[int],optional): The number of results to retrieve. If not specified, defaults to the value set during initialization.\n\nReturns:\n\ndspy.Prediction: Contains the retrieved passages, each represented as adotdictwith along_textattribute.\n\nInternally, the method handles the specifics of preparing the request query to the Azure AI Search service and corresponding payload to obtain the response.\n\nThe function handles the retrieval of the top-k passages based on the provided query.'}, {'level': 2, 'title': 'Sending Retrieval Requests via AzureAISearchRM Client', 'content': 'RecommendedConfigure default RM usingdspy.configure.\n\nThis allows If you want to use something else, you can provide your custom embedding_func, but either the openai_client or embedding_func must be provided.\nIf you need to enable semantic search, either with vector, hybrid, or full text search, then set the\nsemantic_ranker\nflag to True.\nIf\nsemantic_ranker\nis True, always set the\nquery_type\nto QueryType.SEMANTIC and always provide the\nsemantic_configuration_name\n.\nExample of the AzureAISearchRM constructor:\nAzureAISearchRM\n(\nsearch_service_name\n:\nstr\n,\nsearch_api_key\n:\nstr\n,\nsearch_index_name\n:\nstr\n,\nfield_text\n:\nstr\n,\nfield_vector\n:\nOptional\n[\nstr\n]\n=\nNone\n,\nk\n:\nint\n=\n3\n,\nazure_openai_client\n:\nOptional\n[\nopenai\n.\nAzureOpenAI\n]\n=\nNone\n,\nopenai_embed_model\n:\nOptional\n[\nstr\n]\n=\n"text-embedding-ada-002"\n,\nembedding_func\n:\nOptional\n[\nCallable\n]\n=\nNone\n,\nsemantic_ranker\n:\nbool\n=\nFalse\n,\nfilter\n:\nstr\n=\nNone\n,\nquery_language\n:\nstr\n=\n"en-Us"\n,\nquery_speller\n:\nstr\n=\n"lexicon"\n,\nuse_semantic_captions\n:\nbool\n=\nFalse\n,\nquery_type\n:\nOptional\n[\nQueryType\n]\n=\nQueryType\n.\nFULL\n,\nsemantic_configuration_name\n:\nstr\n=\nNone\n,\nis_vector_search\n:\nOptional\n[\nbool\n]\n=\nFalse\n,\nis_hybrid_search\n:\nOptional\n[\nbool\n]\n=\nFalse\n,\nis_fulltext_search\n:\nOptional\n[\nbool\n]\n=\nTrue\n,\nvector_filter_mode\n:\nOptional\n[\nVectorFilterMode\n.\nPRE_FILTER\n]\n=\nNone\n)\nUnder the Hood\nforward(self, query_or_queries: Union[str, List[str]], k: Optional[int] = None) -> dspy.Prediction\nParameters:\nquery_or_queries\n(Union[str, List[str]]): The query or queries to search for.\nk\n(\nOptional[int]\n,\noptional\n): The number of results to retrieve. If not specified, defaults to the value set during initialization.\nReturns:\ndspy.Prediction\n: Contains the retrieved passages, each represented as a\ndotdict\nwith a\nlong_text\nattribute.\nInternally, the method handles the specifics of preparing the request query to the Azure AI Search service and corresponding payload to obtain the response.\nThe function handles the retrieval of the top-k passages based on the provided query.\nSending Retrieval Requests via AzureAISearchRM Client\nRecommended\nConfigure default RM using\ndspy.configure\n.\nThis allows {'url': 'https://dspy.ai/deep-dive/retrieval_models_clients/custom-rm-client/', 'title': 'Custom RM Client - DSPy', 'structure': [{'level': 1, 'title': 'Creating Custom RM Client', 'content': "DSPy provides support for various retrieval modules out of the box likeColBERTv2,AzureCognitiveSearch,Lancedb,Pinecone,Weaviate, etc. Unlike Language Model (LM) modules, creating a custom RM module is much more simple and flexible.\n\nAs of now, DSPy offers 2 ways to create a custom RM: the Pythonic way and the DSPythonic way. We\'ll take a look at both, understand why both are performing the same behavior, and how you can implement each!\nI/O of RM Client\nBefore understanding the implementation, let\'s understand the idea and I/O within RM modules.\nThe\ninput\nto an RM module is either 1) a single query or 2) a list of queries.\nThe\noutput\nis the\ntop-k\npassages per query retrieved from a retrieval model, vector store, or search client.\nConventionally, we simply call the RM module object through the\n__call__\nmethod, inputting the query/queries as argument(s) of the call with the corresponding output returned as a list of strings.\nWe\'ll see how this I/O is essentially same in both methods of implementation but differs in their delivery.\nThe Pythonic Way\nTo account for our RM I/O, we create a class that conducts the retrieval logic, which we implement in the\n__init__\nand\n__call__\nmethods:\nfrom\ntyping\nimport\nList\n,\nUnion\nclass\nPythonicRMClient\n:\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n):\npass\ndef\n__call__\n(\nself\n,\nquery\n:\nUnion\n[\nstr\n,\nList\n[\nstr\n]],\nk\n:\nint\n)\n->\nUnion\n[\nList\n[\nstr\n],\nList\n[\nList\n[\nstr\n]]]:\npass\nInfo\nDon\'t worry about the extensive type-hinting above.\ntyping\nis a package that provides type-definitions for function inputs and outputs.\nUnion\ncovers all possible types of the argument/output. So:\n*\nUnion[str, List[str]]\n: Assigned to\nquery\nto work with a single query string or a list of queries strings.\n*\nUnion[List[str], List[List[str]]]\n: Assigned to the output of\n__call__\nto work with a single query string as a list or a list of multiple query string lists.\nSo let\'s start by implementing\nPythonicRMClient\nfor a local retrieval model hosted on a API with endpoint being\n/\n. We\'ll start by implementing the\n__init__\nmethod, which simply initializes the class attributes,\nurl\nand\nport\n, and attaches the port to the url if present.\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n,\nurl\n:\nstr\n,\nport\n:\nint\n=\nNone\n):\nself\n.\nurl\n=\nf\n`\n{\nurl\n}:{\nport\n}\n`\nif\nport\nelse\nurl\nNow it\'s time to write the retrieval logic in\n__call__\nmethod:\ndef\n__call__\n(\nself\n,\nquery\n:\nstr\n,\nk\n:\nint\n)\n->\nList\n[\nstr\n]:\nparams\n=\n{\n"query"\n:\nquery\n,\n"k"\n:\nk\n}\nresponse\n=\nrequests\n.\nget\n(\nself\n.\nurl\n,\nparams\n=\nparams\n)\nresponse\n=\nresponse\n.\njson\n()[\n"retrieved_passages"\n]\n# List of top-k passages\nreturn\nresponse\nThis serves to represent our API request call to retrieve our list of\ntop-k passages\nwhich we return as the response. Let\'s bring it all together and see how our RM class looks like:\nfrom\ntyping\nimport\nList\nclass\nPythonicRMClient\n:\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n,\nurl\n:\nstr\n,\nport\n:\nint\n=\nNone\n):\nself\n.\nurl\n=\nf\n`\n{\nurl\n}:{\nport\n}\n`\nif\nport\nelse\nurl\ndef\n__call__\n(\nself\n,\nquery\n:\nstr\n,\nk\n:\nint\n)\n->\nList\n[\nstr\n]:\n# Only accept single query input, feel free to modify it to support\nparams\n=\n{\n"query"\n:\nquery\n,\n"k"\n:\nk\n}\nresponse\n=\nrequests\n.\nget\n(\nself\n.\nurl\n,\nparams\n=\nparams\n)\nresponse\n=\nresponse\n.\njson\n()[\n"retrieved_passages"\n]\n# List of top k passages\nreturn\nresponse\nThat\'s all!! This is the most basic way to implement a RM model and mirrors DSP-v1-hosted RM models like\nColBERTv2\nand\nAzureCognitiveSearch\n.\nNow let\'s take a look at how we streamline this process in DSPy!\nThe DSPythonic Way\nThe DSPythonic way mirrors the Pythonic way in maintaining the same input but now returning an object of\ndspy.Prediction\nclass, the standard output format for any DSPy module as we\'ve seen in previous docs. Additionally, this class would now inherit the\ndspy.Retrieve\nclass to maintain state management within the DSPy library.\nSo let\'s implement\n__init__\nand\nforward\nmethod where our class\'s\n__call__\nis calling the\nforward\nmethod as is=:\nimport\ndspy\nfrom\ntyping\nimport\nList\n,\nUnion\n,\nOptional\nclass\nDSPythonicRMClient\n(\ndspy\n.\nRetrieve\n):\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n,\nk\n:\nint\n):\npass\ndef\nforward\n(\nself\n,\nquery\n:\nUnion\n[\nstr\n,\nList\n[\nstr\n]],\nk\n:\nOptional\n[\nstr\n])\n->\ndspy\n.\nPrediction\n:\npass\nUnlike\nPythonicRMClient\n, we initialize\nk\nas part of the initialization call and the\nforward\nmethod will take query/queries as arguments and the\nk\nnumber of retrieved passages as an optional argument.\nk\nis used within the inherited\ndspy.Retrieve\ninitialization when we call\nsuper().__init__()\n.\nWe\'ll be implementing\nDSPythonicRMClient\nfor the same local retrieval model API we used above. We\'ll start by implementing the\n__init__\nmethod, which mirrors the\nPythonicRMClient\n.\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n,\nurl\n:\nstr\n,\nport\n:\nint\n=\nNone\n,\nk\n:\nint\n=\n3\n):\nsuper\n()\n.\n__init__\n(\nk\n=\nk\n)\nself\n.\nurl\n=\nf\n`\n{\nurl\n}:{\nport\n}\n`\nif\nport\nelse\nurl\nWe\'ll now implement the\nforward\nmethod, returning the output as a\ndspy.Prediction\nobject under the\npassage\nattribute which is standard among all the RM modules. The call will default to the defined\nself.k\nargument unless overridden in this call.\ndef\nforward\n(\nself\n,\nquery\n:\nstr\n,\nk\n:\nOptional\n[\nint\n])\n->\ndspy\n.\nPrediction\n:\nparams\n=\n{\n"query"\n:\nquery\n,\n"k"\n:\nk\nif\nk\nelse\nself\n.\nk\n}\nresponse\n=\nrequests\n.\nget\n(\nself\n.\nurl\n,\nparams\n=\nparams\n)\nresponse\n=\nresponse\n.\njson\n()[\n"retrieved_passages"\n]\n# List of top k passages\nreturn\ndspy\n.\nPrediction\n(\npassages\n=\nresponse\n)\nLet\'s bring it all together and see how our RM class looks like:\nimport\ndspy\nfrom\ntyping\nimport\nList\n,\nUnion\n,\nOptional\nclass\nDSPythonicRMClient\n(\ndspy\n.\nRetrieve\n):\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n,\nurl\n:\nstr\n,\nport\n:\nint\n=\nNone\n,\nk\n:\nint\n=\n3\n):\nsuper\n()\n.\n__init__\n(\nk\n=\nk\n)\nself\n.\nurl\n=\nf\n`\n{\nurl\n}:{\nport\n}\n`\nif\nport\nelse\nurl\ndef\nforward\n(\nself\n,\nquery_or_queries\n:\nstr\n,\nk\n:\nOptional\n[\nint\n])\n->\ndspy\n.\nPrediction\n:\nparams\n=\n{\n"query"\n:\nquery_or_queries\n,\n"k"\n:\nk\nif\nk\nelse\nself\n.\nk\n}\nresponse\n=\nrequests\n.\nget\n(\nself\n.\nurl\n,\nparams\n=\nparams\n)\nresponse\n=\nresponse\n.\njson\n()[\n"retrieved_passages"\n]\n# List of top k passages\nreturn\ndspy\n.\nPrediction\n(\npassages\n=\nresponse\n)\nThat\'s all!! This is the way to implement a custom RM model client within DSPy and how more recently-supported RM models like\nQdrantRM\n,\nWeaviateRM\n,\nLancedbRM\n, etc. are implemented in DSPy.\nLet\'s take a look at how we use these retrievers.\nUsing Custom RM Models\nDSPy offers two ways of using custom RM clients: Direct Method and using\ndspy.Retrieve\n.\nDirect Method\nThe most straightforward way to use your custom RM is by directly using its object within the DSPy Pipeline.\nLet\'s take a look at the following pseudocode of a DSPy Pipeline as an example:\nclass\nDSPyPipeline\n(\ndspy\n.\nModule\n):\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n):\nsuper\n()\n.\n__init__\n()\nurl\n=\n"http://0.0.0.0"\nport\n=\n3000\nself\n.\npythonic_rm\n=\nPythonicRMClient\n(\nurl\n=\nurl\n,\nport\n=\nport\n)\nself\n.\ndspythonic_rm\n=\nDSPythonicRMClient\n(\nurl\n=\nurl\n,\nport\n=\nport\n,\nk\n=\n3\n)\n...\ndef\nforward\n(\nself\n,\n*\nargs\n):\n...\npassages_from_pythonic_rm\n=\nself\n.\npythonic_rm\n(\nquery\n)\npassages_from_dspythonic_rm\n=\nself\n.\ndspythonic_rm\n(\nquery\n)\n.\npassages\n...\nThis ensures you retrieve a list of passages from your RM client and can interact with the results within your forward pass in whichever way needed for your pipeline\'s purpose!\nUsing\ndspy.Retrieve\nThis way is more experimental in essence, allowing you to maintain the same pipeline and experiment with different RMs. How? By configuring it!\nimport\ndspy\nlm\n=\n...\nurl\n=\n"http://0.0.0.0"\nport\n=\n3000\n# pythonic_rm = PythonicRMClient(url=url, port=port)\ndspythonic_rm\n=\nDSPythonicRMClient\n(\nurl\n=\nurl\n,\nport\n=\nport\n,\nk\n=\n3\n)\ndspy\n.\nsettings\n.\nconfigure\n(\nlm\n=\nlm\n,\nrm\n=\ndspythonic_rm\n)\nNow, in the pipeline, you just need to use\ndspy.Retrieve\nwhich will use this\nrm\nclient to get the top-k passage for a given query!\nclass\nDSPyPipeline\n(\ndspy\n.\nModule\n):\ndef\n__init__\n(\nself\n):\nsuper\n()\n.\n__init__\n()\nurl\n=\n"http://0.0.0.0"\nport\n=\n3000\nself\n.\nrm\n=\ndspy\n.\nRetrieve\n(\nk\n=\n3\n)\n...\ndef\nforward\n(\nself\n,\n*\nargs\n):\n...\npassages\n=\nself\n.\nrm\n(\nquery\n)\n...\nNow if you\'d like to use a different RM, you can just update the\nrm\nparameter via\ndspy.settings.configure\n.\nHow\ndspy.Retrieve\nuses\nrm\nWhen we call\ndspy.Retrieve\nthe\n__call__\nmethod will execute the\nforward\nmethod as is. In\nforward\n, the top-k passages are received by the\ndsp.retrieveEnsemble\nmethod in\nsearch.py\n.\nIf an\nrm\nis not initialized in\ndsp.settings\n, this would raise an error.', 'last_updated': '2025-04-11T17:40:44.069462'}

{'url': 'https://dspy.ai/deep-dive/retrieval_models_clients/DatabricksRM/', 'title': 'DatabricksRM - DSPy', 'structure': [{'level': 1, 'title': 'DatabricksRM', 'content': ''}, {'level': 3, 'title': 'Constructor', 'content': 'Initialize an instance of theDatabricksRMretriever class, which enables DSPy programs to queryDatabricks Mosaic AI Vector Searchindexes for document retrieval.\n\nDatabricksRM(databricks_index_name:str,databricks_endpoint:Optional[str]=None,databricks_token:Optional[str]=None,columns:Optional[List[str]]=None,filters_json:Optional[str]=None,k:int=3,docs_id_column_name:str="id",text_column_name:str="text",)\n\nParameters:\n\ndatabricks_index_name (str): The name of the Databricks Vector Search Index to query.databricks_endpoint (Optional[str]): The URL of the Databricks Workspace containing\n  the Vector Search Index. Defaults to the value of theDATABRICKS_HOSTenvironment variable.\n  If unspecified, the Databricks SDK is used to identify the endpoint based on the current\n  environment.databricks_token (Optional[str]): The Databricks Workspace authentication token to use\n  when querying the Vector Search Index. Defaults to the value of theDATABRICKS_TOKENenvironment variable. If unspecified, the Databricks SDK is used to identify the token based on\n  the current environment.columns (Optional[List[str]]): Extra column names to include in response, in addition to the\n  document id and text columns specified bydocs_id_column_nameandtext_column_name.filters_json (Optional[str]): A JSON string specifying additional query filters.\n  Example filters:{"id <": 5}selects records that have anidcolumn value\n  less than 5, and{"id >=": 5, "id <": 10}selects records that have anidcolumn value greater than or equal to 5 and less than 10.k (int): The number of documents to retrieve.docs_id_column_name (str): The name of the column in the Databricks Vector Search Index\n  containing document IDs.text_column_name (str): The name of the column in the Databricks Vector Search Index\n  containing document text to retrieve.'}, {'level': 3, 'title': 'Methods', 'content': ''}, {'level': 4, 'title': 'def forward(self, query: Union[str, List[float]], query_type: str = "ANN", filters_json: Optional[str] = None) -> dspy.Prediction:', 'content': 'Retrieve documents from a Databricks Mosaic AI Vector Search Index that are relevant to the\nspecified query.\n\nParameters:\n\nquery (Union[str, List[float]]): The query text or numeric query vector\n  for which to retrieve relevant documents.query_type (str): The type of search query to perform against the\n  Databricks Vector Search Index. Must be either \'ANN\' (approximate nearest neighbor) or \'HYBRID\'\n  (hybrid search).filters_json (Optional[str]): A JSON string specifying additional query filters.\n  Example filters:{"id <": 5}selects records that have anidcolumn value\n  less than 5, and{"id >=": 5, "id <": 10}selects records that have anidcolumn value greater than or equal to 5 and less than 10. If specified, this\n  parameter overrides thefilters_jsonparameter passed to the constructor.\n\nReturns:\n\ndspy.Prediction: Adotdictcontaining retrieved documents. The schema is{\'docs\': List[str], \'doc_ids\': List[Any], extra_columns: List[Dict[str, Any]]}.\n  Thedocsentry contains the retrieved document content.'}, {'level': 3, 'title': 'Quickstart', 'content': 'To retrieve documents using Databricks Mosaic AI Vector Search, you mustcreate a\nDatabricks Mosaic AI Vector Search Indexfirst.\n\nThe following example code demonstrates how to set up a Databricks Mosaic AIDirect Access Vector Search Indexand use theDatabricksRMDSPy retriever module to query the index. The example requires\nthedatabricks-vectorsearchPython library to be installed.\n\nfromdatabricks.vector_search.clientimportVectorSearchClient# Create a Databricks Vector Search Endpointclient=VectorSearchClient()client.create_endpoint(name="your_vector_search_endpoint_name",endpoint_type="STANDARD")# Create a Databricks Direct Access Vector Search Indexindex=client.create_direct_access_index(endpoint_name="your_vector_search_endpoint_name",index_name="your_index_name",primary_key="id",embedding_dimension=1024,embedding_vector_column="text_vector",schema={"id":"int","field2":"str","field3":"float","text_vector":"array<float>"})# Create a DatabricksRM retriever and retrieve the top-3 most relevant documents from the# Databricks Direct Access Vector Search Index corresponding to an example queryretriever=DatabricksRM(databricks_index_name="your_index_name",docs_id_column_name="id",text_column_name="field2",k=3)retrieved_results=DatabricksRM(query="Example query text",query_type="hybrid"))'}], 'full_text': 'DatabricksRM\nConstructor\nInitialize an instance of the\nDatabricksRM\nretriever class, which enables DSPy programs to query\nDatabricks Mosaic AI Vector Search\nindexes for document retrieval.\nDatabricksRM\n(\ndatabricks_index_name\n:\nstr\n,\ndatabricks_endpoint\n:\nOptional\n[\nstr\n]\n=\nNone\n,\ndatabricks_token\n:\nOptional\n[\nstr\n]\n=\nNone\n,\ncolumns\n:\nOptional\n[\nList\n[\nstr\n]]\n=\nNone\n,\nfilters_json\n:\nOptional\n[\nstr\n]\n=\nNone\n,\nk\n:\nint\n=\n3\n,\ndocs_id_column_name\n:\nstr\n=\n"id"\n,\ntext_column_name\n:\nstr\n=\n"text"\n,\n)\nParameters:\ndatabricks_index_name (str)\n: The name of the Databricks Vector Search Index to query.\ndatabricks_endpoint (Optional[str])\n: The URL of the Databricks Workspace containing\n  the Vector Search Index. Defaults to the value of the\nDATABRICKS_HOST\nenvironment variable.\n  If unspecified, the Databricks SDK is used to identify the endpoint based on the current\n  environment.\ndatabricks_token (Optional[str])\n: The Databricks Workspace authentication token to use\n  when querying the Vector Search Index. Defaults to the value of the\nDATABRICKS_TOKEN\nenvironment variable. If unspecified, the Databricks SDK is used to identify the token based on\n  the current environment.\ncolumns (Optional[List[str]])\n: Extra column names to include in response, in addition to the\n  document id and text columns specified by\ndocs_id_column_name\nand\ntext_column_name\n.\nfilters_json (Optional[str])\n: A JSON string specifying additional query filters.\n  Example filters:\n{"id <": 5}\nselects records that have an\nid\ncolumn value\n  less than 5, and\n{"id >=": 5, "id <": 10}\nselects records that have an\nid\ncolumn value greater than or equal to 5 and less than 10.\nk (int)\n: The number of documents to retrieve.\ndocs_id_column_name (str)\n: The name of the column in the Databricks Vector Search Index\n  containing document IDs.\ntext_column_name (str)\n: The name of the column in the Databricks Vector Search Index\n  containing document text to retrieve.\nMethods\ndef forward(self, query: Union[str, List[float]], query_type: str = "ANN", filters_json: Optional[str] = None) -> dspy.Prediction:\nRetrieve documents from a Databricks Mosaic AI Vector Search Index that are relevant to the\nspecified query.\nParameters:\nquery (Union[str, List[float]])\n: The query text or numeric query vector\n  for which to retrieve relevant documents.\nquery_type (str)\n: The type of search query to perform against the\n  Databricks Vector Search Index.
